nodes.name: PD-1
nodes.description: Programmed cell death protein 1 — key immune checkpoint receptor
nodes.body: Expressed on activated T cells, B cells, and NK cells. Binding to PD-L1/PD-L2 delivers inhibitory signals that suppress T-cell proliferation and cytokine production. Upregulated in tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes. Gene: PDCD1.

nodes.name: PD-L1
nodes.description: Programmed death-ligand 1 — expressed on tumor cells to evade immunity
nodes.body: Transmembrane protein expressed on tumor cells, APCs, and epithelial cells. Binds PD-1 to suppress anti-tumor T-cell responses. Upregulated by IFN-gamma. Gene: CD274. Also binds CD80 (B7-1).

nodes.name: CTLA-4
nodes.description: Cytotoxic T-lymphocyte-associated protein 4 — early checkpoint
nodes.body: Competes with CD28 for B7 ligand binding on APCs. Acts primarily in lymph nodes during T-cell priming. Constitutively expressed on Tregs. Gene: CTLA4.

nodes.name: LAG-3
nodes.description: Lymphocyte activation gene 3 — next-gen checkpoint target
nodes.body: Binds MHC class II with higher affinity than CD4. Negatively regulates T-cell proliferation and homeostasis. Synergizes with PD-1 blockade. Gene: LAG3.

nodes.name: CD8+ T-cell
nodes.description: Cytotoxic T lymphocyte — primary anti-tumor effector
nodes.body: Recognizes tumor antigens via MHC-I. Kills tumor cells via perforin/granzyme and Fas/FasL pathways. Exhaustion driven by chronic antigen exposure and checkpoint signaling. Re-invigorated by PD-1 blockade.

nodes.name: Tumor Cell
nodes.description: Malignant cell — upregulates PD-L1 to evade immune destruction
nodes.body: Expresses neoantigens from somatic mutations. Upregulates PD-L1 (constitutively or IFN-gamma-induced) to suppress CD8+ T-cell killing. Higher tumor mutational burden (TMB) correlates with checkpoint inhibitor response.

nodes.name: APC (Dendritic Cell)
nodes.description: Antigen-presenting cell — primes T-cell responses
nodes.body: Captures and presents tumor antigens to naive T cells via MHC-I (cross-presentation) and MHC-II. Expresses B7 (CD80/CD86) co-stimulatory ligands. Critical for initiating anti-tumor immunity.

nodes.name: Treg
nodes.description: Regulatory T cell — suppresses anti-tumor immunity in the TME
nodes.body: CD4+CD25+FoxP3+ cells that suppress effector T-cell function. Enriched in the tumor microenvironment. Constitutively express CTLA-4. CTLA-4 blockade may deplete Tregs via ADCC.

nodes.name: NK Cell
nodes.description: Natural killer cell — innate anti-tumor effector
nodes.body: Kills MHC-I-low tumor cells without prior antigen sensitization. Activated by IL-2, IL-15, IFN-gamma. Expresses PD-1 in the tumor microenvironment. May benefit from checkpoint blockade.

nodes.name: Pembrolizumab (Keytruda)
nodes.description: Anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody — first approved checkpoint inhibitor for many cancers
nodes.body: Humanized IgG4 monoclonal antibody blocking PD-1. Approved for melanoma, NSCLC, HNSCC, Hodgkin lymphoma, urothelial carcinoma, MSI-H tumors, and many more. KEYNOTE trial program.

nodes.name: Nivolumab (Opdivo)
nodes.description: Anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody — often combined with ipilimumab
nodes.body: Fully human IgG4 monoclonal antibody blocking PD-1. Approved for melanoma, NSCLC, RCC, and others. CheckMate trial program. Frequently combined with ipilimumab (anti-CTLA-4).

nodes.name: Atezolizumab (Tecentriq)
nodes.description: Anti-PD-L1 monoclonal antibody
nodes.body: Humanized IgG1 monoclonal antibody targeting PD-L1. Engineered Fc to reduce ADCC/CDC. Approved for NSCLC, urothelial carcinoma, TNBC, HCC, and SCLC. IMpower/IMvigor trial programs.

nodes.name: Ipilimumab (Yervoy)
nodes.description: Anti-CTLA-4 monoclonal antibody — first approved checkpoint inhibitor
nodes.body: Fully human IgG1 monoclonal antibody blocking CTLA-4. First checkpoint inhibitor approved (melanoma, 2011). Enhances T-cell priming in lymph nodes. May deplete intratumoral Tregs via ADCC. Higher irAE rate than anti-PD-1.

nodes.name: Relatlimab (Opdualag component)
nodes.description: Anti-LAG-3 monoclonal antibody — first approved LAG-3 inhibitor
nodes.body: Human IgG4 monoclonal antibody blocking LAG-3. Approved in fixed-dose combination with nivolumab for melanoma (RELATIVITY-047 trial). First LAG-3-targeted therapy to reach market.

nodes.name: TCR Signaling
nodes.description: T-cell receptor signal transduction cascade
nodes.body: Initiated by MHC-peptide complex recognition. Activates ZAP-70, LAT, PLC-gamma, Ras/MAPK, and NF-kB pathways. Co-stimulation (CD28/B7) required for full activation. PD-1 recruits SHP-2 to attenuate proximal TCR signaling.

nodes.name: IFN-gamma Pathway
nodes.description: Interferon gamma signaling — dual role in anti-tumor immunity
nodes.body: Produced by activated CD8+ T-cells and NK cells. Activates JAK1/JAK2-STAT1 in tumor cells. Upregulates MHC-I (enhances recognition) but also PD-L1 (promotes evasion). Loss of IFN-gamma signaling is a resistance mechanism.

nodes.name: PI3K/AKT Pathway
nodes.description: Intracellular survival and proliferation pathway in tumor cells
nodes.body: PTEN loss activates PI3K/AKT in tumor cells, promoting survival, PD-L1 expression, and immunosuppressive cytokine production. Also drives T-cell differentiation and memory formation. Therapeutic target for combination strategies.

nodes.name: Tumor Regression
nodes.description: Desired outcome — tumor shrinkage or complete response
nodes.body: Checkpoint blockade achieves durable responses in a subset of patients. Complete responses possible and often long-lasting. Response rates vary by cancer type (15-60%). Predictive biomarkers: PD-L1 expression, TMB, MSI status, TIL density.

nodes.name: irAE (Immune-related Adverse Events)
nodes.description: Side effects from unleashed immune activity
nodes.body: Autoimmune-like toxicities from checkpoint blockade. Common: dermatitis, colitis, hepatitis, thyroiditis, pneumonitis. Grade 3-4 in 10-30% (anti-PD-1) vs 40-60% (combination). Management: corticosteroids, infliximab, mycophenolate.

nodes.name: Resistance
nodes.description: Primary or acquired resistance to immunotherapy
nodes.body: Primary: low TMB, cold tumor (no TILs), defective antigen presentation, immunosuppressive TME. Acquired: loss of MHC-I, beta-2-microglobulin mutations, JAK1/2 loss-of-function, upregulation of alternative checkpoints (LAG-3, TIM-3, TIGIT).
